text.title: Spec-Kit Three-Step Pipeline (specify → plan → tasks) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Team skips `specify`, hand-writes plan.md, agent ships code. Two model upgrades later the team can't reconstruct intent.
antipattern.root-cause: Plan written without a spec; no durable WHAT/WHY anchor.
antipattern.fix: Restore the order: run `specify` first; commit spec.md; only then re-plan.

antipattern.symptom: Coding agent prompt concatenates spec.md + plan.md + tasks.md. Agent confuses what to do with what to know.
antipattern.fix: Pass only tasks.md as the agent prompt; spec/plan stay as reference docs in context, not instructions.

antipattern.symptom: Two `[P]` tasks both edit `src/models.py`. CI thrashes with merge conflicts.
antipattern.root-cause: `[P]` applied by pattern-match, not file-overlap analysis.
antipattern.fix: Validate `[P]` by static analysis: tasks share no file path. Reject the tag if they do.
